defends: A recoverable point-in-time snapshot of the config tree: the archive is created AND verified (stable sha256, extractable, every in-scope file captured), never assumed.
note: FIXED scope (WORKING_METHOD §1). include_roots '.' = archive the whole target. exclude is applied BOTH to the walk that builds the expected-entry set AND to tar, so the two agree on scope: prior archives (data/backups), .git, node_modules and secret files are kept out. The sentinel is injected ONLY into the temp staging copy during self-test — never into the real target. A live --apply run proves capture by asserting every walked in-scope file is present in the archive listing.
